locator.raw: Laboratory Testing / Urine dipstick paragraph ("The intensity of color changes...")
locator.section: Laboratory Testing / Urine dipstick paragraph ("The intensity of color changes...")

applicability.ageRange: Children (general pediatric screening population); no age band, sex, or GA/PNA stratification specified

laboratory.assayMethod: Colorimetric urine reagent dipstick; primarily detects albuminuria with specificity and sensitivity >99%, not sensitive for non-albumin proteins (method-dependent semiquantitative color grade)

locator.raw: Evaluation / spot UPr/Cr paragraph ("If the UPr/Cr ratio is 0.2 or less...")
locator.section: Evaluation / spot UPr/Cr paragraph ("If the UPr/Cr ratio is 0.2 or less...")

applicability.ageRange: Children >2 years (cutoff 0.2); children six to 24 months of age (cutoff 0.5); no sex stratification

laboratory.assayMethod: Spot first-morning urine protein-to-creatinine ratio (excludes postural effect); reliable substitute for 24-hour collection. Ratio value is analyzer/units convention dependent (mass/mass mg/mg)

locator.raw: Laboratory Testing / 24-hour excretion paragraph ("In children, the normal amount of protein...")
locator.section: Laboratory Testing / 24-hour excretion paragraph ("In children, the normal amount of protein...")

applicability.ageRange: Children (general; no age band, sex, or GA/PNA subdivision given for this normal value)

laboratory.assayMethod: 24-hour timed urine protein excretion (total protein, body-surface-area normalized); noted as often impractical/difficult in children

locator.raw: Nephrotic syndrome paragraph ("Nephrotic syndrome is characterized by heavy proteinuria...")
locator.section: Nephrotic syndrome paragraph ("Nephrotic syndrome is characterized by heavy proteinuria...")

applicability.ageRange: Children with nephrotic syndrome (general pediatric; no age/sex band specified in this definition)

laboratory.assayMethod: 24-hour urine protein excretion (mg/m2/day) OR spot protein-to-creatinine ratio; two interchangeable measures given

locator.raw: Introduction / CKD risk paragraph ("The Chronic Kidney Disease in Children study demonstrated...")
locator.section: Introduction / CKD risk paragraph ("The Chronic Kidney Disease in Children study demonstrated...")

laboratory.assayMethod: Urine protein-to-creatinine ratio (mass/mass); prognostic threshold from the CKiD cohort study

locator.section: Abstract / Orthostatic proteinuria and Referral sections

laboratory.assayMethod: not applicable (clinical classification, not a single-analyzer numeric threshold)

locator.section: HEMATURIA > Definitions and Measurement Methods, para 2

laboratory.assayMethod: Method-dependent: urine microscopy of spun (centrifuged) sediment vs uncentrifuged count; UA-with-microscopy is laboratory-dependent per the source. Confirmed only after a positive dipstick.

locator.section: HEMATURIA > Classification, final para

applicability.ageRange: Children with microscopic hematuria (both sexes); menses exclusion implies post-menarchal adolescents among the confounders. Age band not further specified.

locator.section: HEMATURIA > Nonglomerular Causes of Hematuria, para 1

applicability.ageRange: Age-banded, both sexes: children >2 years (cutoff >0.2); infants <6 months (acceptable <0.8); infants 6-12 months (acceptable <0.6). Infants have higher calcium excretion + lower urine creatinine per source.

laboratory.assayMethod: Spot urine calcium/creatinine ratio (mg/mg). Not stated as analyzer-dependent.

locator.raw: PROTEINURIA > Definition, para 1 (and Measurement Methods for the >2 yr U p/c normal)
locator.section: PROTEINURIA > Definition, para 1 (and Measurement Methods for the >2 yr U p/c normal)

applicability.ageRange: Age-banded, both sexes: children >2 years (U p/c normal <0.2); infants/children 6-24 months (normal U p/c <0.5); neonates and infants allowed up to 300 mg/m2 per day.

laboratory.assayMethod: Spot (single) urine protein/creatinine ratio, preferably first-morning; 24-hr collection alternative. Dipstick detects albumin (surrogate), not LMW proteins. U p/c falsely elevated with low creatinine excretion; underestimated with concentrated high-creatinine urine.
